explanation: Applied the fused AdamW optimization in the model's optimizer निर्माण path. This keeps training behavior the same while allowing PyTorch to use the single-kernel fused AdamW implementation on CUDA-capable systems, reducing optimizer overhead and memory bandwidth usage.

changes.description: Enable fused AdamW when constructing the optimizer, guarded by CUDA availability.
changes.new_snippet: optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(optim_groups, lr=train_config.learning_rate, betas=train_config.betas, fused=torch.cuda.is_available()) # profine: fused_adamw

warnings: Fused AdamW requires a PyTorch build that supports fused optimizers and a CUDA device; on unsupported setups, PyTorch may raise an error when constructing the optimizer.

extra_file_edits.diff:
--- mingpt/model.py+++ mingpt/model.py (optimized)@@ -250,7 +250,7 @@             {"params": [param_dict[pn] for pn in sorted(list(decay))], "weight_decay": train_config.weight_decay},
             {"params": [param_dict[pn] for pn in sorted(list(no_decay))], "weight_decay": 0.0},
         ]
-        optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(optim_groups, lr=train_config.learning_rate, betas=train_config.betas)
+        optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(optim_groups, lr=train_config.learning_rate, betas=train_config.betas, fused=torch.cuda.is_available()) # profine: fused_adamw
         return optimizer
 
     def forward(self, idx, targets=None):
